title: JIT IR translation — message send and selective receive
purpose: Message passing (send operator !) and selective receive are the core BEAM concurrency primitives. JIT-compiling these to native code eliminates interpreter dispatch overhead for the send instruction and the receive loop. Send compiles to a runtime helper call. Receive compiles to a loop that scans the process mailbox with pattern matching on each message, with a timeout mechanism for receive-after clauses. These operations interact with the scheduler (yield during receive wait) and the process mailbox.
task: Implement IR translation for message send and selective receive opcodes in crates/beamr/src/jit/ir_message.rs. The send operator compiles to a jit_send_message runtime helper. Selective receive (loop_rec, loop_rec_end, wait, wait_timeout, remove_message) compiles to a loop structure with runtime helpers for mailbox scanning. Receive loops include reduction checks and yield points.

requirements.title: Message send IR — send operator
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate the send operator (!) into Cranelift IR that calls jit_send_message(process, dest_pid, message) runtime helper. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a GC safepoint before the send (the message may be deep-copied to the recipient's heap). THE SYSTEM SHALL return the sent message as the result (BEAM semantics: send returns the message).
acceptance: translate_send(builder, emitter, dest, message, result_dest) emits safepoint + jit_send_message call
jit_send_message(process, dest_pid: u64, message: u64) -> u64 sends and returns the message
GC safepoint root set includes both dest_pid and message
Result register receives the message term (not a success/fail indicator)
Test: compiled send(Pid, hello) -> message received by target process
Test: compiled send to dead pid -> message dropped silently (BEAM semantics)

requirements.title: Selective receive loop IR — mailbox scanning
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate loop_rec and loop_rec_end into a Cranelift IR loop that scans the process mailbox. loop_rec loads the next message from the mailbox save pointer and branches to the body (match attempt) or the wait label (mailbox exhausted). loop_rec_end advances the save pointer to the next message and jumps back to loop_rec. THE SYSTEM SHALL call jit_receive_peek(process) and jit_receive_next(process) runtime helpers for mailbox interaction.
acceptance: translate_loop_rec(builder, emitter, fail_label) emits: jit_receive_peek call, branch to fail if exhausted
translate_loop_rec_end(builder, emitter, loop_label) emits: jit_receive_next call, jump to loop_label
jit_receive_peek(process) -> Option<u64> returns the current message or None
jit_receive_next(process) advances the mailbox save pointer
Test: compiled receive matches first message in mailbox
Test: compiled receive skips non-matching messages and matches later message

requirements.title: Message removal and receive completion
requirements.spec: WHEN a message matches a receive clause, THE SYSTEM SHALL translate remove_message into IR that calls jit_receive_accept(process) to remove the matched message from the mailbox. THE SYSTEM SHALL ensure the message Term is accessible from the destination register before removal. THE SYSTEM SHALL track the message as a GC root if it is live across any safepoint in the receive clause body.
acceptance: translate_remove_message(builder, emitter) emits jit_receive_accept call
jit_receive_accept(process) removes the current message from the mailbox
The message value remains valid in the destination register after removal
Message Term is in the safepoint root set if used after the accept call
Test: receive hello -> remove_message -> message no longer in mailbox

requirements.title: Receive timeout — wait and wait_timeout
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL translate wait into IR that suspends the process until a new message arrives (calls jit_receive_wait(process) which yields to the scheduler). THE SYSTEM SHALL translate wait_timeout into IR that sets a timeout and suspends until either a message arrives or the timeout expires. WHEN the timeout expires, THE SYSTEM SHALL branch to the timeout clause label. THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a reduction check at the wait point (yield to scheduler).
acceptance: translate_wait(builder, emitter, loop_label) emits jit_receive_wait + reduction check + jump to loop
translate_wait_timeout(builder, emitter, timeout_value, timeout_label, loop_label) emits timeout setup + wait
jit_receive_wait(process) -> WaitResult::NewMessage or WaitResult::Timeout
NewMessage: jump back to loop_rec for re-scan
Timeout: branch to timeout_label
Test: receive with after 0 -> immediately executes timeout clause when mailbox empty
Test: receive with message already in mailbox -> no wait, immediate match

requirements.title: Opcode dispatch wiring for message opcodes
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL update the main opcode dispatch in compiler.rs to route send, loop_rec, loop_rec_end, wait, wait_timeout, remove_message, and timeout opcodes to ir_message.rs translations instead of emitting deopt exits. THE SYSTEM SHALL leave recv_marker_bind, recv_marker_clear, and recv_marker_use opcodes as deopt (ref-matching optimisation is out of scope).
acceptance: send, loop_rec, loop_rec_end, wait, wait_timeout, remove_message route to ir_message.rs
recv_marker opcodes still deopt to interpreter
Test: function with send + receive compiles without deopt

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement receive optimisation (ref-matching fast path) — basic linear scan only
SHALL NOT implement distributed message send — local pid only (remote send deoptimises to interpreter)
SHALL NOT implement process_info(messages) from compiled code — deoptimise to interpreter
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
ir_message.rs is under 500 lines of production code
Send emits GC safepoint before message copy
Receive loop includes reduction checks at wait points
